essence: David weighs whether Signal Studio should act on current incident info now or wait, proposing a temporary CRUD incident endpoint (like company/site/user/participant) as a stopgap for creating a holding/starter incident, and asks about v5.7 status.

actions.text: Decide whether to build a temporary CRUD incident endpoint as a stopgap for holding/starter incidents

essence: David plans a security-focused cleanup of his Claude Code setup — verifying downloaded repos against original sources of truth, and auditing prior agent activity for any exfiltration or prompt-injection exposure before implementing a new report.

actions.text: Verify downloaded repos are original (not reshaped) and identify the trusted source of truth
Audit prior session activity for exfiltration or prompt-injection exposure

essence: David plans to run his second laptop on a new Anthropic account (maybe $100 plan) testing Hermes as memory + agent-calling harness with two jobs: continually improving AngelEye from incoming session data, and drafting the spec for a new Agent Workflow Builder harness that both plugs into and embeds within the SupportSignal app (via git pull + rsync of Claude session files).

actions.text: Set up second laptop with new Anthropic account and test Hermes as memory/agent harness
Draft requirements and spec for a new Agent Workflow Builder harness (plug-in + embedded-in-SupportSignal variants)
Configure git pull of SupportSignal app code and rsync-over-SSH of Claude session files to second laptop

essence: David rejects running a whole company in agents (no control, too costly) and instead has Paperclip AI read only new AngelEye conversation data to detect structural workflow patterns — e.g. BMAD left-to-right agent calls vs Ralph Wiggum loops as horizontal waves of ~10 agents.

Facing Signal Studio↔SupportSignal sync doubts, David frames an Epic 0 story: a deep entity-retrieval API with an entity-only vs entity-plus-children flag so Signal Studio can debug by comparing what it sends against what comes back.

actions.text: Write feature request for deep entity retrieval with child-entity flag (Epic 0 story)

essence: David finds pushed participant data marked 'synced' in Signal Studio doesn't match the SupportSignal production database, questions how sync status is verified and a suspicious raw-ID artifact in URLs, and suggests centralizing sync verification on the push dashboard instead of per-page 'push all pending' buttons.

actions.text: Centralize sync verification on the push dashboard rather than duplicating controls on every listing page

essence: Directive to git commit and push the code changes, with a clarifying question on whether the task needs web-server access or Git alone.

actions.text: Git commit and push current code changes

essence: Quick instruction to check the new build errors (background-check related) and see if they can be resolved easily.

actions.text: Review new build errors and assess if easily resolvable

essence: David probes a record-resync failure (possible privilege issue) — is it detectable, repeatable, affecting other entities, a component bug — and asks that the steps be logged for Lisa, wondering if Lisa can watch an issue for a few rounds then dismiss it.

actions.text: Log the resync-failure steps as a learning for Lisa to track

essence: David and Lars agree to daily 30-min skill-building sessions (~14:00 David's time, starting next day) with a shared 'relay-lars' Dropbox folder as shared memory; David shares his multi-machine relay architecture, the learning that Claude accounts must not be shared across computers (share the brain, not the account), his three relay systems including the Paperclip relay folder, and pending approved-but-not-yet-granted access to Anthropic's unreleased Mythos model (beta Mar 31–Apr 14, 15h feedback required) which he plans to throw at rebuilding Agent Workflow Builder.

actions.text: Start daily 30-minute sessions with Lars from tomorrow at agreed time
Create shared 'relay-lars' Dropbox folder as shared memory and confirm which email/Dropbox account (moving to appydave.com)
Send Lars an invoice tonight
Use Mythos beta access (if granted) to rebuild Agent Workflow Builder as a standalone project

essence: David records a monthly plan walkthrough for Lars: get Ansible/Tailscale/SyncThing/Dropbox infra solid on Lars's machines, teach BMAD agent building for growth hacking, later set up Agent Workflow Builder with predicates/observations feeding AngelEye, run simple Paperclip automations, and hold regular (near-daily) 30-min single-problem calls plus one BMAD deep dive.

actions.text: Verify Tailscale and SyncThing relay/peer-to-peer folders working on Lars's machines
Teach Lars BMAD agent building for his growth-hacking workflow, then configure Agent Workflow Builder
Integrate predicate/observation views into the AngelEye interface

essence: David tries to reach the Paperclip dev server from his other machine via Tailscale (macbook-pro:3100) — it loads but demands a fresh login and returns 'invalid claim', leaving cross-machine Paperclip access unresolved.
Rather than briefing the other Codex session verbally, David directs that its shared document be updated with all learnings so far plus a short handover summary for it to read.

actions.text: Update the other Codex session's shared document with current learnings and provide a concise handover

essence: During workflow 5.4, David hands Signal Studio instructions for pushing/updating participant-data screens after SupportSignal changes, wants Lisa to keep the story/docs updated so questions aren't re-asked, and decides to document a token/permission exposure risk in the brain doc and defer revocation to the end since revoking now would break the current terminal.

actions.text: Review Signal Studio push instructions against recent participant-data changes
Document the token/permission exposure risk in the brain document; defer revocation until end of work
Have Lisa keep story and documentation updated as stories progress
